surprise_log.observation: An NWS Taunton-Massachusetts weather-forecast-office bomb cyclone nor'easter coastal-flood-and-storm-tide warning surfaced on the household-side third-floor home-library corner-bay window-bench upholstered briefing-tablet Wednesday-mid-afternoon: the NWS Taunton-Massachusetts forecast-office in coordination with the NWS Boston-and-Caribou-and-Gray-and-New-York-and-Mount-Holly-and-Wakefield-and-Sterling weather-forecast-office cluster has issued a bomb cyclone explosive-cyclogenesis nor'easter coastal-flood-and-storm-tide warning across the New-England-and-Mid-Atlantic coastal corridor spanning Kennebunk-Maine-and-Portsmouth-New-Hampshire-and-Boston-Massachusetts-and-Providence-Rhode-Island-and-New-Haven-Connecticut-and-Manhattan-and-Long-Island-New-York-and-Asbury-Park-and-Atlantic-City-New-Jersey gauge-station fleet following a sub-twenty-four-hour 988-millibar-to-944-millibar central-pressure drop pattern qualifying the system under the Sanders-and-Gyakum explosive-cyclogenesis bombogenesis threshold band. The bomb cyclone nor'easter coastal-flood-and-storm-tide warning carries a four-to-six-foot above-mean-high-water coastal-flood inundation forecast across the per-county tidal-station fleet with the per-county storm-tide-and-wave-runup combined hazard band pulling the per-county A-zone-and-V-zone NFIP coastal-flood-zone fleet into a mandatory-evacuation pre-staging posture across the per-county coastal-evacuation corridor. The bomb cyclone nor'easter wave-and-wind-and-surge picture pulls the per-station NDBC offshore-buoy fleet thirty-five-to-fifty-foot significant-wave-height open-ocean swell band plus the per-coast nearshore breaking-wave run-up nine-to-fourteen-foot band plus the per-coast peak-storm-surge five-to-eight-foot above-normal-astronomical-tide band plus the per-coast Category-Two-equivalent sustained-coastal-winds sixty-five-to-eighty-mile-per-hour band with one-hundred-five-mile-per-hour gust band across the per-county Beach-Hazards-Statement-and-Coastal-Flood-Advisory escalation corridor. The bomb cyclone nor'easter coastal-flood-and-storm-tide warning closes with a RISK-PROBABILITY-CASCADE forecast picture covering graded deterministic-and-ensemble probability bands across the per-county per-hazard impact-band threshold-exceedance tiles: per-station coastal-flood inundation-exceedance ninety-five-percent-confidence band on three-foot-above-mean-high-water threshold + seventy-five-percent-confidence band on four-foot threshold + fifty-percent-confidence band on five-foot threshold + twenty-five-percent-confidence band on six-foot threshold + five-percent-confidence tail band on seven-foot-plus threshold pulled from the per-station Stevens-Institute-NYHOPS-and-NOAA-PORTS-and-Stevens-NJCFLOOD ensemble surge-and-tide hindcast-and-forecast cycle; per-coast nearshore breaking-wave run-up exceedance ninety-five-percent-confidence band on six-foot threshold + seventy-five-percent-confidence band on nine-foot threshold + fifty-percent-confidence band on eleven-foot threshold + twenty-five-percent-confidence band on thirteen-foot threshold pulled from the per-coast SWAN-and-WAVEWATCHIII nearshore wave-spectrum ensemble cycle; per-county Category-Two-equivalent sustained-coastal-winds exceedance ninety-five-percent-confidence band on fifty-mile-per-hour threshold + seventy-five-percent-confidence band on sixty-mile-per-hour threshold + fifty-percent-confidence band on seventy-mile-per-hour threshold + twenty-five-percent-confidence band on eighty-mile-per-hour threshold + five-percent-confidence tail band on one-hundred-mile-per-hour gust threshold pulled from the per-county NWS-and-ECMWF-and-HRRR-and-GFS ensemble wind-field nowcast-and-forecast cycle; per-county storm-tide-and-coastal-flood inundation footprint exceedance ninety-five-percent-confidence band on per-county A-zone-only footprint + seventy-five-percent-confidence band on per-county A-zone-plus-V-zone footprint + fifty-percent-confidence band on per-county A-and-V-and-shaded-X-zone combined footprint + twenty-five-percent-confidence band on per-county A-and-V-and-X-and-recurring-loss-property combined footprint pulled from the per-county FEMA-flood-hazard-layer-and-USACE-coastal-storm-surge-model-and-per-county-floodplain-administrator parcel-level cycle; per-coast nor'easter wave-and-wind-and-surge composite-hazard footprint exceedance fifty-percent-confidence band on per-coast moderate-impact-corridor footprint + twenty-five-percent-confidence band on per-coast major-impact-corridor footprint + five-percent-confidence tail band on per-coast catastrophic-impact-corridor footprint pulled from the per-coast NWS-Storm-Prediction-Center-and-Ocean-Prediction-Center composite-hazard-corridor ensemble cycle. The bomb cyclone nor'easter coastal-flood-and-storm-tide warning carries a per-county pre-landfall watch-to-warning escalation cadence pull plus a per-county post-landfall storm-tide-recession-and-coastal-damage-survey rollup pass plus a per-coast NWS-Ocean-Prediction-Center post-event re-analysis hindcast pull plus a per-county FEMA-IA-Preliminary-Damage-Assessment activation pathway across the per-county pre-disaster-declaration-and-disaster-declaration cycle on the per-county Stafford-Act-Section-401-and-Section-403-and-Section-407 pathway covering the per-county Public-Assistance-and-Individual-Assistance categories on the per-county pre-impact-resource-staging-and-mutual-aid-EMAC activation surface.
